cities.name: Rize
cities.tagline: Turkey's tea country — misty highlands, green valleys, and the famous Ayder plateau.
cities.summary: Rize is the Black Sea's green heart. Don't base in the city itself — head to the highlands. Ayder Yaylası is the famous highland village for scenery. Çamlıhemşin is the traditional riverside gateway. Fırtına Valley is for serious nature travelers.

areas.name: Ayder Yaylası (Plateau)
areas.oneLiner: The famous misty highland village at 1350m — wooden chalets, thermal baths, waterfalls.
pros: One of the most photogenic villages in Turkey
cons: Can be crowded with domestic tourists in August

areas.name: Çamlıhemşin & Fırtına Valley
areas.oneLiner: The river-valley approach to Ayder — old stone bridges, Zilkale castle, raftable rivers.
pros: Zilkale Castle perched on the cliff

areas.name: Rize City Center
areas.oneLiner: Urban base for tea-garden visits — simple hotels, seafront, and the famous tea factory tours.
pros: Tea garden day tours start here

hotels.name: Ayder Doga Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Wooden-lodge hotel on the Ayder plateau with valley views and a highland spa — the atmospheric choice.

hotels.name: Ayder Avusor Butik Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Beautifully restored mountain lodge, excellent local breakfast, consistently top-rated in the plateau.

hotels.name: Ayder Hasimoglu Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Long-running plateau hotel with larger rooms — good for families exploring the highlands.

hotels.name: Firtina Pansiyon
hotels.whyStay: Simple family-run pension on the Fırtına river — eat fresh trout on the terrace, sleep to the river's sound.

hotels.name: Dedeman Rize
hotels.whyStay: Reliable branded 5-star in central Rize with sea views — easiest airport base.

hotels.name: Rize Park Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Good-value central hotel near the seafront and main square, modern rooms.

hotels.name: Ayder Villa de Pelit Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Highest-rated boutique lodge on the plateau (9.1 on Booking) — wooden chalet rooms, rooftop terrace, locally-loved breakfast.

hotels.name: Ayder Koru Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Long-running plateau hotel surrounded by forest with a proper restaurant and 24-hour front desk — reliable when others are booked.

hotels.name: Ayder Palazzo Resort
hotels.whyStay: 4-star with the most modern rooms in Ayder — mountain-view balconies, garden terrace, the polished option in a rustic plateau.

hotels.name: Çathan Art Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Riverside budget pick in Çamlıhemşin near Zilkale — basic but characterful, good base for the Fırtına Valley without Ayder prices.

faqs.q: Should I stay in Rize city or the plateau?
faqs.a: The plateau (Ayder) — that's why you come. Use Rize city only as a 1-night arrival/departure stop if needed.

faqs.q: When does it rain in Rize?
faqs.a: All year, honestly — the Black Sea coast is Turkey's rainiest region. Expect some rain even in August. Pack waterproof shoes and a light rain jacket.

cities.name: Ankara
cities.tagline: Turkey's capital — business travel, Anıtkabir, and a growing food scene.
cities.summary: Ankara is Turkey's political and administrative capital. It's not a leisure destination most foreigners target — but it's essential for business travel and for visitors to Anıtkabir (Atatürk's mausoleum). Stay in Çankaya for the embassy/upscale district, or Kızılay for young/affordable/walkable.

areas.name: Çankaya (Kavaklıdere / Gaziosmanpaşa)
areas.oneLiner: The diplomatic district — embassies, designer boutiques, and the best international hotels.
pros: Close to Tunalı Hilmi restaurant street

areas.name: Kızılay
areas.oneLiner: Central Ankara — student cafés, government buildings, the metro hub.

hotels.name: JW Marriott Hotel Ankara
hotels.whyStay: One of Ankara's flagship 5-stars — excellent service, great breakfast, connected to a mall.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Ankara
hotels.whyStay: Large conference-friendly 5-star with panoramic views, pool, and spa.

hotels.name: Divan Çankaya
hotels.whyStay: Mid-sized 4-star in a quiet Çankaya lane — consistently good reviews from business travelers.

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hotel Ankara
hotels.whyStay: Central Kızılay location near the metro — reliable brand, modern rooms.

hotels.name: Hotel Ickale
hotels.whyStay: Well-reviewed budget hotel in central Kızılay — basic but clean with strong wifi.

hotels.name: Sheraton Ankara Hotel & Convention Center
hotels.whyStay: Cylindrical Çankaya landmark with 9.3 location score — indoor pool, jazz bar, walking distance to Tunalı Hilmi.

hotels.name: Wyndham Ankara
hotels.whyStay: Modern 5-star in Söğütözü business district, indoor pool and Turkish bath, 10 minutes from Anıtkabir.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Ankara by IHG
hotels.whyStay: Reliable IHG 5-star with the city's largest meeting facilities — go-to for conferences and government delegations.

hotels.name: Latanya Hotel Ankara
hotels.whyStay: Long-established Çankaya hotel on Gazi Mustafa Kemal Bulvarı — walkable to ministries, Kızılay and the embassy district.

hotels.name: Days Hotel by Wyndham Ankara Çankaya
hotels.whyStay: Solid mid-tier brand in upper Çankaya near Segmenler Park — free parking, restaurant, much cheaper than the diplomatic-row 5-stars.

faqs.a: For leisure travelers: one night is enough, focused on Anıtkabir, the Anatolian Civilisations Museum, and Haci Bayram. Most foreign tourists skip it unless they're doing business.

cities.name: Gaziantep
cities.tagline: Turkey's food capital — UNESCO gastronomy city and home of real baklava.
cities.summary: Gaziantep (locally Antep) is where serious Turkish food tourism happens. It's not beach, not cave hotels — it's eating your way through one of the world's great food cities. Stay in the center to walk to the bazaars, baklava houses, and the extraordinary Zeugma Mosaic Museum.

areas.name: Gaziantep Old City
areas.oneLiner: The historic hammam, copper market, and castle district — where the food tours start.
pros: Best baklava houses at your door

areas.name: Şahinbey & Modern Center
areas.oneLiner: The modern commercial side — malls, office hotels, easier for business travel.
cons: 10–15 min drive to the old city and food scene

hotels.name: The Anatolian Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Restored old-city mansion with a courtyard — walk to baklava and the covered bazaar.

hotels.name: Tugcan Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Modern 5-star near the commercial district with an indoor pool and reliable breakfast.

hotels.name: Divan Gaziantep
hotels.whyStay: Dependable Divan-brand 5-star with rooftop restaurant and city views.

hotels.name: Grand Hotel Gaziantep
hotels.whyStay: Reliable mid-range near the center — good value in a city that's mostly about the food, not the hotel.

hotels.name: Hampton by Hilton Gaziantep
hotels.whyStay: Modern budget brand — consistent standards for a food-focused trip where the hotel is just a base.

faqs.q: Is Gaziantep worth flying to just for food?
faqs.a: Yes, genuinely. A weekend here (with a Zeugma Museum visit) is one of Turkey's best short trips. Do a food tour on day one.
Yes. It's a large, established Turkish city. The southeastern location means you should pay routine attention but it's not a destination with notable safety concerns.

cities.name: Bursa
cities.tagline: The first Ottoman capital — thermal baths, ski slopes, and İskender kebab at its source.
cities.summary: Bursa is easy to reach from Istanbul (2 hours by ferry + road) and gives you three experiences: Ottoman heritage in the old city, thermal spa hotels in Çekirge, and Turkey's best ski resort on Uludağ mountain.

areas.name: Çekirge
areas.oneLiner: The thermal spa district — every hotel has its own hot spring, old Ottoman baths nearby.

areas.name: Bursa City Center (Osmangazi)
areas.oneLiner: The old Ottoman heart — Ulu Mosque, Green Mosque, covered bazaar, İskender kebab houses.

areas.name: Uludağ (Ski Zone)
areas.oneLiner: Turkey's most accessible ski resort — chair lifts, slopes, and mountain-chalet hotels at 2000m.

hotels.name: Kervansaray Termal Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Historic thermal hotel built over original Ottoman hot springs — authentic experience.

hotels.name: Grand Thermal Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Large thermal resort with multiple indoor pools, family-friendly, good value.

hotels.name: Çelik Palas Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Historic landmark hotel (Atatürk slept here) with a stunning domed thermal hall and excellent spa.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Bursa Convention Center & Thermal Spa
hotels.whyStay: Modern 5-star with thermal pools and central Bursa access — reliable choice.

hotels.name: Almira Hotel
hotels.whyStay: Central 4-star in downtown Bursa — walk to Ulu Mosque and the covered bazaar.

hotels.name: Monte Baia Uludağ
hotels.whyStay: Ski-in hotel at 2000m with a huge indoor pool, kids' ski school, good breakfast.

hotels.name: Grand Yazıcı Hotel & Ski Center
hotels.whyStay: One of Uludağ's biggest ski resort hotels with half-board packages.

faqs.q: Is Bursa a good day trip from Istanbul?
faqs.a: Long day. Fast ferry + 1-hour bus gets you there in ~3 hours each way. Better as a 1-2 night break. In winter, pair with Uludağ skiing for a proper weekend.

faqs.q: Which hammam should I try in Bursa?
faqs.a: Çekirge's Eski Kaplıca (15th century) for history, or your hotel's private thermal pool for comfort. Both tap the same natural hot springs.
